title: wolftpm-src always defines WOLFSSL_USER_SETTINGS; wolftpm-sys has conditional logic
description: wolftpm-src/build.rs unconditionally sets build.define('WOLFSSL_USER_SETTINGS', None) with a comment 'always define'. But wolftpm-sys/build.rs conditionally defines it (use_user_settings flag based on whether user_settings.h is detected). These are inconsistent. In practice wolftpm-src compiles the C library while wolftpm-sys compiles the shim and runs bindgen — if the shim defines WOLFSSL_USER_SETTINGS unconditionally but bindgen does not (or vice versa), the C preprocessor sees different views of wolfSSL headers. Verify they are consistent in all build configurations or align them explicitly.
